name: WooCommerce
one_liner: Open-source WooCommerce is a WordPress e-commerce plugin whose built-in REST API exposes store data (products, orders, customers, coupons) to external systems.
primary_auth: API key (Consumer Key + Consumer Secret pair, sent via HTTP Basic over HTTPS or OAuth 1.0a over HTTP)
access_notes: WooCommerce is a free, open-source WordPress plugin. On any self-hosted install, an admin generates REST API keys under WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API (Read/Write) with no payment or approval. Credentials are a Consumer Key + Consumer Secret tied to a WordPress user. Requires the operator to have their own WooCommerce/WordPress instance.
api_notes: REST API covers products, product variations, orders, refunds, customers, coupons, reports, tax rates, shipping zones/methods, payment gateways, settings, and system status - well over 100 operations. Full reference at woocommerce.github.io/woocommerce-rest-api-docs.
mcp_notes: WooCommerce (Automattic) ships an official MCP integration, documented at developer.woocommerce.com/docs/features/mcp/, currently in developer preview. It uses the @automattic/mcp-wordpress-remote local proxy translating MCP to HTTP and authenticates via WooCommerce REST API keys. Numerous community MCP servers also exist (techspawn, iOSDevSK, etc.).
main_blocker: No API-side gate; the only prerequisite is having a WooCommerce store instance to generate keys against (no hosted/central API - each merchant's own site is the endpoint).

evidence.claim: API keys (Consumer Key + Consumer Secret) are created in WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API with Read/Write permissions; WooCommerce is a free open-source plugin so REST API access requires no paid extension.
WooCommerce ships an official Model Context Protocol integration (developer preview) using API-key auth and the Automattic WordPress remote proxy.
The REST API reference documents broad coverage across products, orders, customers, coupons, reports, and webhooks.

open_questions: OAuth 1.0a is used for non-HTTPS sites while HTTPS sites use Basic auth with the key pair; exact auth choice depends on the store's configuration. The official MCP is in developer preview and its API may change.
